label: Schwaan_2_non_myelinating
note: Label uses 'Schwaan' spelling matching the atlas annotation exactly.
evidence: Reynolds et al. (2021) identify two Schwann cell subtypes in adult human skin: myelinating (Schwaan_1) and non-myelinating (Schwaan_2). Non-myelinating Schwann cells (also termed Remak Schwann cells) form Remak bundles that ensheath multiple unmyelinated C-fibres. They are defined by expression of NGFR (p75NTR), GFAP, GLUL (glutamine synthetase), APOD, FABP7, and SMOC2, with lower expression of myelin genes MPZ and MBP compared to myelinating Schwann cells.
